title: Ihara-Bass Scaling: Graph Family Comparison + Convergence Analysis
key_discovery: The Ihara-Bass determinant det(I - e^{-s} T_P) with prime bond lengths {ln p : p ≤ P} converges toward ξ(s) as P grows. At P=47 (15 primes), 168/169 zeros matched at 0.53% mean error with density ratio 1.03 at T=120. The zero pattern is an intrinsic property of the prime set, largely independent of graph topology once connectivity is sufficient.

C_n_all.note: Cycle graphs produce no critical-line zeros

density_convergence.note: Density ratio = N_Ihara(T) / N_zeta(T). Ratio 1.0 = perfect R-vM match.

T350.interpretation: 34% undercounting — 15 primes insufficient at this height

euler_product_interpretation.formula: D_P(s) = det(I - e^{-s} · T_P) where T_P is Hashimoto of graph with bonds {ln p : p ≤ P}
euler_product_interpretation.evidence: 112 zeros at P=47, same pattern across K_5, K_11, K_13, Reeds_23 — topology-independent

ihara_xi_identity.interpretation: D_P(s) = P(s) · ξ(s) where P(s) is a polynomial determined by Hashimoto eigenvalues not on the critical circle
